source_intake_record.raw_question_text: In the diagram, $K L M N$ is a unit square. Arcs of radius one unit are drawn using each of the four corners of the square as centres. The arcs centred at $K$ and $L$ intersect at $Q$; the arcs centred at $M$ and $N$ intersect at $P$. What is the length of $P Q$ ? <image1>

image_dependency: The problem relies on spatial relations among a square, four arcs, and their intersection points P and Q
asset_registry_record shows an existing image and registry_passed=true
multi_step: Solving requires using the unit square structure together with radius-1 arc intersections
The solver must determine or characterize Q and P from two different pairs of arc intersections before finding PQ
The target answer is an exact radical expression, indicating nontrivial geometric derivation rather than a single direct read-off
verifiability: The answer is a precise closed-form value: '\sqrt{3}-1'
The same value appears as one of the multiple-choice options, enabling straightforward answer checking
A coordinate-geometry or Euclidean derivation could independently verify the result
